description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
A bladed, hilted weapon. Specifically designed for cutting or thrusting. +1 to Attack.
Increases your BODY stat to 12 (adjusting your HP and Wound Threshold and Death Save)
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A concealable one-handed Ranged Weapon. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated.
An artificial eye. Choose Kiroshi Optics!
Must be paired. Provides immunity to dazzling effects (like flashbangs).
Must be paired. Ignore any vision penalties due to lighting or fog/smoke.
A base interface for audio enhancements.
Built in agent. Audio only but can be linked with other cybergear.
Provides immunity to deafness or sudden loud noises.
Provides immunity to inhalable gases or fumes.
Provides +2 to Resist Torture/Drugs checks.
Armor Item for Subdermal Armor Cyberware.
Double ablation to armor.
Access to nervous system.
Plugs in the wrist or head to allow you to connect to machines/systems hands-free.
Chipware. Removes Seriously Wounded penalties.
A concealable exotic net launcher. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated. (See Black Chrome pgs. 20-21 for more details)
Net for the net launcher that grapples the target on hit. (See Black Chrome Pg. 20-21 for more details)

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 5x/Special Boss Threat - An isolated, specialized, high-level agent that will likely pose a significant threat even without any backup.

Corp-Wide Ability - Arasaka Counterintelligence: Arasaka agents can appear anywhere, at any time, for any reason, with motives that might completely elude all but them. Every Arasaka agent placed in a scene can be equipped with up to 1,000eb of extra gear at the GM's discretion; additionally, they're fully aware of the exact identities and true appearance of every individual in a scene, along with their personal histories and recent activities. Lastly, once per initiative round, a combination of agility, active camoflauge and deceptive movements allows an Arasaka agent to move in impossible fashion - instead of moving, they may be moved to any space on the board which is accessible to them, within spaces = double their MOVE characteristic of their previous location.

All Arasaka agents can dodge attacks using their technical skill.

Ability - Corporate Hunter: Driven over the edge into cyberpsychosis, Hunters are assigned tasks and serve with utter devotion - no pain, no fear, just the studious, single-minded intent to kill. When entering a scene, they choose a target. When attacking that target, 5s count as 6s for the purposes of critical injury, they receive +3 to all checks made against the target (including dodging attacks), and may attempt a facedown against them at any time without using an action.

Extra Gear - It should be assumed that Arasaka Hunters have access to virtually anything they'd need to do their jobs, regardless of price. It should also be assumed that they would not part with this gear lightly.

Want to hear a story? Once upon a time, in 2022, there was a tiny island in the Pacific without a name. somewhere in Micronesia. This little island guarded a trade route between Japan and the Philippines, one Militech decided to take for themselves. Arasaka didn't like that. Six months after the occupation, Militech had lost every man they put on the island, plus the reinforcements, and never saw one enemy soldier. When it was over, Arasaka issued a press release: one Hunter had been deployed, told to do whatever it took.

After that, Militech stopped trying to take territory and just focused on asset destruction. Makes you think, eh? Anyways, I'm sure you'll be fine.

Token frame by Charniecia Gardner - find her on Instagram at runicwolfroxxa (link).
